evidence_quotes.quote: it's just a license that I rent from them, which they can take away at any time without the explanation nor refund

providers like "steam" hide the fact that the account that I created doesn't belong to me, that it's just a license that I rent from them, which they can take away at any time without the explanation nor refund

the whole 'think about the kids' thing forcing everyone now to submit their real id's to the untrusted third parties that for sure will get all of that users data 'stolen' will lead to heavy push back
Banks visa/master card or any third party shouldn't dictate/censor or even delete the media due to some activists from a different continent that think fictional characters are real.

paragraphs.text: Hello there, first of all, publishers must stop killing games when they end the service support for them, games are digital pieces of art and history that should be stored and available for everyone to enjoy at any time after their support has ended. Hundreds of games over the years because lost media due to such bad publisher practices. 1.any digital media games/music/movies that we paid for belongs to us, and must be accessible and functional/playable at any time with no restrictions from service providers.(if restricted then the consumers must get full refund from a service provider cdkey bought outside the platform also must count + ingame cosmetics) Same goes for the consumers accounts where such media is stored, providers like "steam" hide the fact that the account that I created doesn't belong to me, that it's just a license that I rent from them, which they can take away at any time without the explanation nor refund (i assume that it is their some weird protection from consumers, if they ever get sued due to their anti consumer practices) 2. Banks visa/master card or any third party shouldn't dictate/censor or even delete the media due to some activists from a different continent that think fictional characters are real. Again it's parents responsibility (not the government or third parties) to check what their kid does on the internet, media shouldn't be restricted for everyone just because modern parents don't know how to parent their kids! The whole 'think about the kids' thing forcing everyone now to submit their real id's to the untrusted third parties that for sure will get all of that users data 'stolen' will lead to heavy push back, so i hope that anyone reading this understands that when internets gets censored this hard, people won't accept it kindly, like they did with migrants and other eu's scams.
